2052 useSkipTable()
2053
2054 func rfind*(s: string, sub: char, start: Natural = 0, last = -1): int {.rtl,
2055 extern: "nsuRFindChar".} =
2056 ## Searches for `sub` in `s` inside range `start..last` (both ends included)
2057 ## in reverse -- starting at high indexes and moving lower to the first
2058 ## character or `start`. If `last` is unspecified, it defaults to `s.high`
2059 ## (the last element).
2060 ##
2061 ## Searching is case-sensitive. If `sub` is not in `s`, -1 is returned.
2062 ## Otherwise the index returned is relative to `s[0]`, not `start`.
2063 ## Subtract `start` from the result for a `start`-origin index.
2064 ##
2065 ## See also:
2066 ## * `find func<#find,string,char,Natural,int>`_
2067 let last = if last == -1: s.high else: last
2068 for i in countdown(last, start):
2069 if sub == s[i]: return i
2070 return -1
2071
2072 func rfind*(s: string, chars: set[char], start: Natural = 0, last = -1): int {.
2073 rtl, extern: "nsuRFindCharSet".} =
2074 ## Searches for `chars` in `s` inside range `start..last` (both ends
2075 ## included) in reverse -- starting at high indexes and moving lower to the
2076 ## first character or `start`. If `last` is unspecified, it defaults to
2077 ## `s.high` (the last element).
2078 ##
2079 ## If `s` contains none of the characters in `chars`, -1 is returned.
2080 ## Otherwise the index returned is relative to `s[0]`, not `start`.
2081 ## Subtract `start` from the result for a `start`-origin index.
2082 ##
2083 ## See also:
2084 ## * `find func<#find,string,set[char],Natural,int>`_
2085 let last = if last == -1: s.high else: last
2086 for i in countdown(last, start):
2087 if s[i] in chars: return i
2088 return -1
2089
2090 func rfind*(s, sub: string, start: Natural = 0, last = -1): int {.rtl,
2091 extern: "nsuRFindStr".} =
2092 ## Searches for `sub` in `s` inside range `start..last` (both ends included)
2093 ## included) in reverse -- starting at high indexes and moving lower to the
2094 ## first character or `start`. If `last` is unspecified, it defaults to
2095 ## `s.high` (the last element).
2096 ##
2097 ## Searching is case-sensitive. If `sub` is not in `s`, -1 is returned.
2098 ## Otherwise the index returned is relative to `s[0]`, not `start`.
2099 ## Subtract `start` from the result for a `start`-origin index.
2100 ##
2101 ## See also:
2102 ## * `find func<#find,string,string,Natural,int>`_
2103 if sub.len == 0:
2104 let rightIndex: Natural = if last < 0: s.len else: last
2105 return max(start, rightIndex)
2106 if sub.len > s.len - start:
2107 return -1
2108 let last = if last == -1: s.high else: last
2109 result = 0
2110 for i in countdown(last - sub.len + 1, start):
2111 for j in 0..sub.len-1:
2112 result = i
2113 if sub[j] != s[i+j]:
2114 result = -1
2115 break
2116 if result != -1: return
2117 return -1
2118
2119
2120 func count*(s: string, sub: char): int {.rtl, extern: "nsuCountChar".} =
2121 ## Counts the occurrences of the character `sub` in the string `s`.
2122 ##
2123 ## See also:
2124 ## * `countLines func<#countLines,string>`_
2125 result = 0
2126 for c in s:
2127 if c == sub: inc result
2128
2129 func count*(s: string, subs: set[char]): int {.rtl,
2130 extern: "nsuCountCharSet".} =
2131 ## Counts the occurrences of the group of character `subs` in the string `s`.
2132 ##
2133 ## See also:
2134 ## * `countLines func<#countLines,string>`_
2135 doAssert card(subs) > 0
2136 result = 0
2137 for c in s:
2138 if c in subs: inc result
2139
2140 func count*(s: string, sub: string, overlapping: bool = false): int {.rtl,
2141 extern: "nsuCountString".} =
2142 ## Counts the occurrences of a substring `sub` in the string `s`.
2143 ## Overlapping occurrences of `sub` only count when `overlapping`
2144 ## is set to true (default: false).
2145 ##
2146 ## See also:
2147 ## * `countLines func<#countLines,string>`_
2148 doAssert sub.len > 0
2149 result = 0
2150 var i = 0
2151 while true:
2152 i = s.find(sub, i)
2153 if i < 0: break
2154 if overlapping: inc i
2155 else: i += sub.len
2156 inc result
2157
2158 func countLines*(s: string): int {.rtl, extern: "nsuCountLines".} =
2159 ## Returns the number of lines in the string `s`.
2160 ##
2161 ## This is the same as `len(splitLines(s))`, but much more efficient
2162 ## because it doesn't modify the string creating temporary objects. Every
2163 ## `character literal <manual.html#lexical-analysis-character-literals>`_
2164 ## newline combination (CR, LF, CR-LF) is supported.
2165 ##
2166 ## In this context, a line is any string separated by a newline combination.
2167 ## A line can be an empty string.
2168 ##
2169 ## See also:
2170 ## * `splitLines func<#splitLines,string>`_
2171 runnableExamples:
2172 doAssert countLines("First line\l and second line.") == 2
2173 result = 1
2174 var i = 0
2175 while i < s.len:
2176 case s[i]
2177 of '\c':
2178 if i+1 < s.len and s[i+1] == '\l': inc i
2179 inc result
2180 of '\l': inc result
2181 else: discard
2182 inc i
2183
2184
2185 46 func contains*(s, sub: string): bool =
2186 ## Same as `find(s, sub) >= 0`.
2187 ##
2188 ## See also:
2189 ## * `find func<#find,string,string,Natural,int>`_
2190
2/4
✗ Branch 0 not taken.
✓ Branch 1 taken 23 times.
✗ Branch 2 not taken.
✓ Branch 3 taken 23 times.
 46 return find(s, sub) >= 0
2191
2192 23 func contains*(s: string, chars: set[char]): bool =
2193 ## Same as `find(s, chars) >= 0`.
2194 ##
2195 ## See also:
2196 ## * `find func<#find,string,set[char],Natural,int>`_
2197 ✗ return find(s, chars) >= 0
2198
2199 ✗ func replace*(s, sub: string, by = ""): string {.rtl,
2200 extern: "nsuReplaceStr".} =
2201 ## Replaces every occurrence of the string `sub` in `s` with the string `by`.
2202 ##
2203 ## See also:
2204 ## * `find func<#find,string,string,Natural,int>`_
2205 ## * `replace func<#replace,string,char,char>`_ for replacing
2206 ## single characters
2207 ## * `replaceWord func<#replaceWord,string,string,string>`_
2208 ## * `multiReplace func<#multiReplace,string,varargs[]>`_ for substrings
2209 ## * `multiReplace func<#multiReplace,openArray[char],varargs[]>`_ for single characters
2210 ✗ result = ""
2211 ✗ let subLen = sub.len
2212 ✗ if subLen == 0:
2213 result = s
2214 ✗ elif subLen == 1:
2215 ✗ # when the pattern is a single char, we use a faster
2216 # char-based search that doesn't need a skip table:
2217 let c = sub[0]
2218 let last = s.high
2219 var i = 0
2220 ✗ while true:
2221 ✗ let j = find(s, c, i, last)
2222 ✗ if j < 0: break
2223 ✗ add result, substr(s, i, j - 1)
2224 ✗ add result, by
2225 ✗ i = j + subLen
2226 # copy the rest:
2227 ✗ add result, substr(s, i)
2228 else:
2229 var a = initSkipTable(sub)
2230 let last = s.high
2231 ✗ var i = 0
2232 ✗ while true:
2233 ✗ let j = find(a, s, sub, i, last)
2234 ✗ if j < 0: break
2235 ✗ add result, substr(s, i, j - 1)
2236 ✗ add result, by
2237 ✗ i = j + subLen
2238 # copy the rest:
2239 ✗ add result, substr(s, i)
2240
2241 func replace*(s: string, sub, by: char): string {.rtl,
2242 extern: "nsuReplaceChar".} =
2243 ## Replaces every occurrence of the character `sub` in `s` with the character
2244 ## `by`.
2245 ##
2246 ## Optimized version of `replace <#replace,string,string,string>`_ for
2247 ## characters.
2248 ##
2249 ## See also:
2250 ## * `find func<#find,string,char,Natural,int>`_
2251 ## * `replaceWord func<#replaceWord,string,string,string>`_
2252 ## * `multiReplace func<#multiReplace,string,varargs[]>`_ for substrings
2253 ## * `multiReplace func<#multiReplace,openArray[char],varargs[]>`_ for single characters
2254 result = newString(s.len)
2255 var i = 0
2256 while i < s.len:
2257 if s[i] == sub: result[i] = by
2258 else: result[i] = s[i]
2259 inc(i)
2260
2261 func replaceWord*(s, sub: string, by = ""): string {.rtl,
2262 extern: "nsuReplaceWord".} =
2263 ## Replaces every occurrence of the string `sub` in `s` with the string `by`.
2264 ##
2265 ## Each occurrence of `sub` has to be surrounded by word boundaries
2266 ## (comparable to `\b` in regular expressions), otherwise it is not
2267 ## replaced.
2268 if sub.len == 0: return s
2269 const wordChars = {'a'..'z', 'A'..'Z', '0'..'9', '_', '\128'..'\255'}
2270 result = ""
2271 var a = initSkipTable(sub)
2272 var i = 0
2273 let last = s.high
2274 let sublen = sub.len
2275 if sublen > 0:
2276 while true:
2277 var j = find(a, s, sub, i, last)
2278 if j < 0: break
2279 # word boundary?
2280 if (j == 0 or s[j-1] notin wordChars) and
2281 (j+sub.len >= s.len or s[j+sub.len] notin wordChars):
2282 add result, substr(s, i, j - 1)
2283 add result, by
2284 i = j + sublen
2285 else:
2286 add result, substr(s, i, j)
2287 i = j + 1
2288 # copy the rest:
2289 add result, substr(s, i)
2290
2291 func multiReplace*(s: string, replacements: varargs[(string, string)]): string =
2292 ## Same as `replace<#replace,string,string,string>`_, but specialized for
2293 ## doing multiple replacements in a single pass through the input string.
2294 ##
2295 ## `multiReplace` scans the input string from left to right and replaces the
2296 ## matching substrings in the same order as passed in the argument list.
2297 ##
2298 ## The implications of the order of scanning the string and matching the
2299 ## replacements:
2300 ## - In case of multiple matches at a given position, the earliest
2301 ## replacement is applied.
2302 ## - Overlaps are not handled. After performing a replacement, the scan
2303 ## continues from the character after the matched substring. If the
2304 ## resulting string then contains a possible match starting in a newly
2305 ## placed substring, the additional replacement is not performed.
2306 ##
2307 ## If the resulting string is not longer than the original input string,
2308 ## only a single memory allocation is required.
2309 ##
2310 runnableExamples:
2311 # Swapping occurrences of 'a' and 'b':
2312 doAssert multireplace("abba", [("a", "b"), ("b", "a")]) == "baab"
2313
2314 # The second replacement ("ab") is matched and performed first, the scan then
2315 # continues from 'c', so the "bc" replacement is never matched and thus skipped.
2316 doAssert multireplace("abc", [("bc", "x"), ("ab", "_b")]) == "_bc"
2317 result = newStringOfCap(s.len)
2318 var i = 0
2319 var fastChk: set[char] = {}
2320 for sub, by in replacements.items:
2321 if sub.len > 0:
2322 # Include first character of all replacements
2323 fastChk.incl sub[0]
2324 while i < s.len:
2325 block sIteration:
2326 # Assume most chars in s are not candidates for any replacement operation
2327 if s[i] in fastChk:
2328 for sub, by in replacements.items:
2329 if sub.len > 0 and s.continuesWith(sub, i):
2330 add result, by
2331 inc(i, sub.len)
2332 break sIteration
2333 # No matching replacement found
2334 # copy current character from s
2335 add result, s[i]
2336 inc(i)
2337
2338 func multiReplace*(s: openArray[char]; replacements: varargs[(set[char], char)]): string {.noinit.} =
2339 ## Performs multiple character replacements in a single pass through the input.
2340 ##
2341 ## `multiReplace` scans the input `s` from left to right and replaces
2342 ## characters based on character sets, applying the first matching replacement
2343 ## at each position. Useful for sanitizing or transforming strings with
2344 ## predefined character mappings.
2345 ##
2346 ## The order of the `replacements` matters:
2347 ## - First matching replacement is applied
2348 ## - Subsequent replacements are not considered for the same character
2349 ##
2350 ## See also:
2351 ## - `multiReplace(s: string; replacements: varargs[(string, string)]) <#multiReplace,string,varargs[]>`_,
2352 runnableExamples:
2353 const WinSanitationRules = [
2354 ({'\0'..'\31'}, ' '),
2355 ({'"'}, '\''),
2356 ({'/', '\\', ':', '|'}, '-'),
2357 ({'*', '?', '<', '>'}, '_'),
2358 ]
2359 # Sanitize a filename with Windows-incompatible characters
2360 const file = "a/file:with?invalid*chars.txt"
2361 doAssert file.multiReplace(WinSanitationRules) == "a-file-with_invalid_chars.txt"
2362 result = newStringUninit(s.len)
2363 for i in 0..<s.len:
2364 var nextChar = s[i]
2365 for subs, by in replacements.items:
2366 if nextChar in subs:
2367 nextChar = by
2368 break
2369 result[i] = nextChar
2370
2371 func insertSep*(s: string, sep = '_', digits = 3): string {.rtl,
2372 extern: "nsuInsertSep".} =
2373 ## Inserts the separator `sep` after `digits` characters (default: 3)
2374 ## from right to left.
2375 ##
2376 ## Even though the algorithm works with any string `s`, it is only useful
2377 ## if `s` contains a number.
2378 runnableExamples:
2379 doAssert insertSep("1000000") == "1_000_000"
2380 result = newStringOfCap(s.len)
2381 let hasPrefix = isDigit(s[s.low]) == false
2382 var idx: int = 0
2383 if hasPrefix:
2384 result.add s[s.low]
2385 for i in (s.low + 1)..s.high:
2386 idx = i
2387 if not isDigit(s[i]):
2388 result.add s[i]
2389 else:
2390 break
2391 let partsLen = s.len - idx
2392 var L = (partsLen-1) div digits + partsLen
2393 result.setLen(L + idx)
2394 var j = 0
2395 dec(L)
2396 for i in countdown(partsLen-1, 0):
2397 if j == digits:
2398 result[L + idx] = sep
2399 dec(L)
2400 j = 0
2401 result[L + idx] = s[i + idx]
2402 inc(j)
2403 dec(L)
2404
2405 func escape*(s: string, prefix = "\"", suffix = "\""): string {.rtl,
2406 extern: "nsuEscape".} =
2407 ## Escapes a string `s`.
2408 ##
2409 ## .. note:: The escaping scheme is different from
2410 ## `system.addEscapedChar`.
2411 ##
2412 ## * replaces `'\0'..'\31'` and `'\127'..'\255'` by `\xHH` where `HH` is its hexadecimal value
2413 ## * replaces ``\`` by `\\`
2414 ## * replaces `'` by `\'`
2415 ## * replaces `"` by `\"`
2416 ##
2417 ## The resulting string is prefixed with `prefix` and suffixed with `suffix`.
2418 ## Both may be empty strings.
2419 ##
2420 ## See also:
2421 ## * `addEscapedChar proc<system.html#addEscapedChar,string,char>`_
2422 ## * `unescape func<#unescape,string,string,string>`_ for the opposite
2423 ## operation
2424 result = newStringOfCap(s.len + s.len shr 2)
2425 result.add(prefix)
2426 for c in items(s):
2427 case c
2428 of '\0'..'\31', '\127'..'\255':
2429 add(result, "\\x")
2430 add(result, toHex(ord(c), 2))
2431 of '\\': add(result, "\\\\")
2432 of '\'': add(result, "\\'")
2433 of '\"': add(result, "\\\"")
2434 else: add(result, c)
2435 add(result, suffix)
2436
2437 func unescape*(s: string, prefix = "\"", suffix = "\""): string {.rtl,
2438 extern: "nsuUnescape".} =
2439 ## Unescapes a string `s`.
2440 ##
2441 ## This complements `escape func<#escape,string,string,string>`_
2442 ## as it performs the opposite operations.
2443 ##
2444 ## If `s` does not begin with `prefix` and end with `suffix` a
2445 ## ValueError exception will be raised.
2446 result = newStringOfCap(s.len)
2447 var i = prefix.len
2448 if not s.startsWith(prefix):
2449 raise newException(ValueError,
2450 "String does not start with: " & prefix)
2451 while true:
2452 if i >= s.len-suffix.len: break
2453 if s[i] == '\\':
2454 if i+1 >= s.len:
2455 result.add('\\')
2456 break
2457 case s[i+1]:
2458 of 'x':
2459 inc i, 2
2460 var c = 0
2461 i += parseutils.parseHex(s, c, i, maxLen = 2)
2462 result.add(chr(c))
2463 dec i, 2
2464 of '\\':
2465 result.add('\\')
2466 of '\'':
2467 result.add('\'')
2468 of '\"':
2469 result.add('\"')
2470 else:
2471 result.add("\\" & s[i+1])
2472 inc(i, 2)
2473 else:
2474 result.add(s[i])
2475 inc(i)
2476 if not s.endsWith(suffix):
2477 raise newException(ValueError,
2478 "String does not end in: " & suffix)
2479
2480 func validIdentifier*(s: string): bool {.rtl, extern: "nsuValidIdentifier".} =
2481 ## Returns true if `s` is a valid identifier.
2482 ##
2483 ## A valid identifier starts with a character of the set `IdentStartChars`
2484 ## and is followed by any number of characters of the set `IdentChars`.
2485 runnableExamples:
2486 doAssert "abc_def08".validIdentifier
2487 result = false
2488 if s.len > 0 and s[0] in IdentStartChars:
2489 for i in 1..s.len-1:
2490 if s[i] notin IdentChars: return false
2491 return true
2492
2493
2494 # floating point formatting:
2495 when not defined(js):
2496 func c_snprintf(buf: cstring, n: csize_t, frmt: cstring): cint {.header: "<stdio.h>",
2497 importc: "snprintf", varargs.}
2498
2499 type
2500 FloatFormatMode* = enum
2501 ## The different modes of floating point formatting.
2502 ffDefault, ## use the shorter floating point notation
2503 ffDecimal, ## use decimal floating point notation
2504 ffScientific ## use scientific notation (using `e` character)
2505
2506 47 func formatBiggestFloat*(f: BiggestFloat, format: FloatFormatMode = ffDefault,
2507 precision: range[-1..32] = 16;
2508 decimalSep = '.'): string {.rtl, extern: "nsu$1".} =
2509 ## Converts a floating point value `f` to a string.
2510 ##
2511 ## If `format == ffDecimal` then precision is the number of digits to
2512 ## be printed after the decimal point.
2513 ## If `format == ffScientific` then precision is the maximum number
2514 ## of significant digits to be printed.
2515 ## `precision`'s default value is the maximum number of meaningful digits
2516 ## after the decimal point for Nim's `biggestFloat` type.
2517 ##
2518 ## If `precision == -1`, it tries to format it nicely.
2519 runnableExamples:
2520 let x = 123.456
2521 doAssert x.formatBiggestFloat() == "123.4560000000000"
2522 doAssert x.formatBiggestFloat(ffDecimal, 4) == "123.4560"
2523 doAssert x.formatBiggestFloat(ffScientific, 2) == "1.23e+02"
2524 when nimvm:
2525 discard "implemented in the vmops"
2526 else:
2527 when defined(js):
2528 var precision = precision
2529 if precision == -1:
2530 # use the same default precision as c_snprintf
2531 precision = 6
2532 var res: cstring
2533 case format
2534 of ffDefault:
2535 {.emit: "`res` = `f`.toString();".}
2536 of ffDecimal:
2537 {.emit: "`res` = `f`.toFixed(`precision`);".}
2538 of ffScientific:
2539 {.emit: "`res` = `f`.toExponential(`precision`);".}
2540 result = $res
2541 if 1.0 / f == -Inf:
2542 # JavaScript removes the "-" from negative Zero, add it back here
2543 result = "-" & $res
2544 for i in 0 ..< result.len:
2545 # Depending on the locale either dot or comma is produced,
2546 # but nothing else is possible:
2547 if result[i] in {'.', ','}: result[i] = decimalSep
2548 else:
2549 const floatFormatToChar: array[FloatFormatMode, char] = ['g', 'f', 'e']
2550 var
2551 frmtstr {.noinit.}: array[0..5, char]
2552 buf {.noinit.}: array[0..2500, char]
2553 47 L: cint
2554 47 frmtstr[0] = '%'
2555
1/2
✓ Branch 0 taken 47 times.
✗ Branch 1 not taken.
 47 if precision >= 0:
2556 47 frmtstr[1] = '#'
2557 47 frmtstr[2] = '.'
2558 47 frmtstr[3] = '*'
2559 47 frmtstr[4] = floatFormatToChar[format]
2560 47 frmtstr[5] = '\0'
2561 47 L = c_snprintf(cast[cstring](addr buf), csize_t(2501), cast[cstring](addr frmtstr), precision, f)
2562 47 else:
2563 ✗ frmtstr[1] = floatFormatToChar[format]
2564 ✗ frmtstr[2] = '\0'
2565 ✗ L = c_snprintf(cast[cstring](addr buf), csize_t(2501), cast[cstring](addr frmtstr), f)
2566
2/4
✓ Branch 0 taken 47 times.
✗ Branch 1 not taken.
✗ Branch 2 not taken.
✓ Branch 3 taken 47 times.
 47 result = newString(L)
2567 188 for i in 0 ..< L:
2568 # Depending on the locale either dot or comma is produced,
2569 # but nothing else is possible:
2570
4/6
✗ Branch 0 not taken.
✓ Branch 1 taken 188 times.
✓ Branch 2 taken 141 times.
✓ Branch 3 taken 47 times.
✗ Branch 4 not taken.
✓ Branch 5 taken 141 times.
 188 if buf[i] in {'.', ','}: result[i] = decimalSep
2571
5/10
✓ Branch 0 taken 47 times.
✗ Branch 1 not taken.
✗ Branch 2 not taken.
✓ Branch 3 taken 47 times.
✓ Branch 4 taken 141 times.
✗ Branch 5 not taken.
✗ Branch 6 not taken.
✓ Branch 7 taken 141 times.
✗ Branch 8 not taken.
✓ Branch 9 taken 141 times.
 188 else: result[i] = buf[i]
2572 47 when defined(windows):
2573 # VS pre 2015 violates the C standard: "The exponent always contains at
2574 # least two digits, and only as many more digits as necessary to
2575 # represent the exponent." [C11 §7.21.6.1]
2576 # The following post-processing fixes this behavior.
2577 if result.len > 4 and result[^4] == '+' and result[^3] == '0':
2578 result[^3] = result[^2]
2579 result[^2] = result[^1]
2580 result.setLen(result.len - 1)
2581
2582 47 func formatFloat*(f: float, format: FloatFormatMode = ffDefault,
2583 precision: range[-1..32] = 16; decimalSep = '.'): string {.
2584 rtl, extern: "nsu$1".} =
2585 ## Converts a floating point value `f` to a string.
2586 ##
2587 ## If `format == ffDecimal` then precision is the number of digits to
2588 ## be printed after the decimal point.
2589 ## If `format == ffScientific` then precision is the maximum number
2590 ## of significant digits to be printed.
2591 ## `precision`'s default value is the maximum number of meaningful digits
2592 ## after the decimal point for Nim's `float` type.
2593 ##
2594 ## If `precision == -1`, it tries to format it nicely.
2595 runnableExamples:
2596 let x = 123.456
2597 doAssert x.formatFloat() == "123.4560000000000"
2598 doAssert x.formatFloat(ffDecimal, 4) == "123.4560"
2599 doAssert x.formatFloat(ffScientific, 2) == "1.23e+02"
2600
2601
1/2
✗ Branch 0 not taken.
✓ Branch 1 taken 47 times.
 47 result = formatBiggestFloat(f, format, precision, decimalSep)
2602 47
2603 func trimZeros*(x: var string; decimalSep = '.') =
2604 ## Trim trailing zeros from a formatted floating point
2605 ## value `x` (must be declared as `var`).
2606 ##
2607 ## This modifies `x` itself, it does not return a copy.
2608 runnableExamples:
2609 var x = "123.456000000"
2610 x.trimZeros()
2611 doAssert x == "123.456"
2612
2613 let sPos = find(x, decimalSep)
2614 if sPos >= 0:
2615 var last = find(x, 'e', start = sPos)
2616 last = if last >= 0: last - 1 else: high(x)
2617 var pos = last
2618 while pos >= 0 and x[pos] == '0': dec(pos)
2619 if pos > sPos: inc(pos)
2620 if last >= pos:
2621 x.delete(pos..last)
2622
2623 type
2624 BinaryPrefixMode* = enum ## The different names for binary prefixes.
2625 bpIEC, # use the IEC/ISO standard prefixes such as kibi
2626 bpColloquial # use the colloquial kilo, mega etc
2627
2628 func formatSize*(bytes: int64,
2629 decimalSep = '.',
2630 prefix = bpIEC,
2631 includeSpace = false): string =
2632 ## Rounds and formats `bytes`.
2633 ##
2634 ## By default, uses the IEC/ISO standard binary prefixes, so 1024 will be
2635 ## formatted as 1KiB. Set prefix to `bpColloquial` to use the colloquial
2636 ## names from the SI standard (e.g. k for 1000 being reused as 1024).
2637 ##
2638 ## `includeSpace` can be set to true to include the (SI preferred) space
2639 ## between the number and the unit (e.g. 1 KiB).
2640 ##
2641 ## See also:
2642 ## * `strformat module<strformat.html>`_ for string interpolation and formatting
2643 runnableExamples:
2644 doAssert formatSize((1'i64 shl 31) + (300'i64 shl 20)) == "2.293GiB"
2645 doAssert formatSize((2.234*1024*1024).int) == "2.233MiB"
2646 doAssert formatSize(4096, includeSpace = true) == "4 KiB"
2647 doAssert formatSize(4096, prefix = bpColloquial, includeSpace = true) == "4 kB"
2648 doAssert formatSize(4096) == "4KiB"
2649 doAssert formatSize(5_378_934, prefix = bpColloquial, decimalSep = ',') == "5,129MB"
2650
2651 # It doesn't needs Zi and larger units until we use int72 or larger ints.
2652 const iecPrefixes = ["", "Ki", "Mi", "Gi", "Ti", "Pi", "Ei"]
2653 const collPrefixes = ["", "k", "M", "G", "T", "P", "E"]
2654
2655 let lg2 = if bytes == 0:
2656 0
2657 else:
2658 when hasWorkingInt64:
2659 fastLog2(bytes)
2660 else:
2661 fastLog2(int32 bytes)
2662 let matchedIndex = lg2 div 10
2663 # Lower bits that are smaller than 0.001 when `bytes` is converted to a real number and added prefix, are discard.
2664 # Then it is converted to float with round down.
2665 let discardBits = (lg2 div 10 - 1) * 10
2666
2667 var prefixes: array[7, string]
2668 if prefix == bpColloquial:
2669 prefixes = collPrefixes
2670 else:
2671 prefixes = iecPrefixes
2672
2673 let fbytes = if lg2 < 10: bytes.float elif lg2 < 20: bytes.float / 1024.0 else: (bytes shr discardBits).float / 1024.0
2674 result = formatFloat(fbytes, format = ffDecimal, precision = 3,
2675 decimalSep = decimalSep)
2676 result.trimZeros(decimalSep)
2677 if includeSpace:
2678 result &= " "
2679 result &= prefixes[matchedIndex]
2680 result &= "B"
2681
2682 func formatEng*(f: BiggestFloat,
2683 precision: range[0..32] = 10,
2684 trim: bool = true,
2685 siPrefix: bool = false,
2686 unit: string = "",
2687 decimalSep = '.',
2688 useUnitSpace = false): string =
2689 ## Converts a floating point value `f` to a string using engineering notation.
2690 ##
2691 ## Numbers in of the range -1000.0<f<1000.0 will be formatted without an
2692 ## exponent. Numbers outside of this range will be formatted as a
2693 ## significand in the range -1000.0<f<1000.0 and an exponent that will always
2694 ## be an integer multiple of 3, corresponding with the SI prefix scale k, M,
2695 ## G, T etc for numbers with an absolute value greater than 1 and m, μ, n, p
2696 ## etc for numbers with an absolute value less than 1.
2697 ##
2698 ## The default configuration (`trim=true` and `precision=10`) shows the
2699 ## **shortest** form that precisely (up to a maximum of 10 decimal places)
2700 ## displays the value. For example, 4.100000 will be displayed as 4.1 (which
2701 ## is mathematically identical) whereas 4.1000003 will be displayed as
2702 ## 4.1000003.
2703 ##
2704 ## If `trim` is set to true, trailing zeros will be removed; if false, the
2705 ## number of digits specified by `precision` will always be shown.
2706 ##
2707 ## `precision` can be used to set the number of digits to be shown after the
2708 ## decimal point or (if `trim` is true) the maximum number of digits to be
2709 ## shown.
2710 ##
2711 ## ```nim
2712 ## formatEng(0, 2, trim=false) == "0.00"
2713 ## formatEng(0, 2) == "0"
2714 ## formatEng(0.053, 0) == "53e-3"
2715 ## formatEng(52731234, 2) == "52.73e6"
2716 ## formatEng(-52731234, 2) == "-52.73e6"
2717 ## ```
2718 ##
2719 ## If `siPrefix` is set to true, the number will be displayed with the SI
2720 ## prefix corresponding to the exponent. For example 4100 will be displayed
2721 ## as "4.1 k" instead of "4.1e3". Note that `u` is used for micro- in place
2722 ## of the greek letter mu (μ) as per ISO 2955. Numbers with an absolute
2723 ## value outside of the range 1e-18<f<1000e18 (1a<f<1000E) will be displayed
2724 ## with an exponent rather than an SI prefix, regardless of whether
2725 ## `siPrefix` is true.
2726 ##
2727 ## If `useUnitSpace` is true, the provided unit will be appended to the string
2728 ## (with a space as required by the SI standard). This behaviour is slightly
2729 ## different to appending the unit to the result as the location of the space
2730 ## is altered depending on whether there is an exponent.
2731 ##
2732 ## ```nim
2733 ## formatEng(4100, siPrefix=true, unit="V") == "4.1 kV"
2734 ## formatEng(4.1, siPrefix=true, unit="V") == "4.1 V"
2735 ## formatEng(4.1, siPrefix=true) == "4.1" # Note lack of space
2736 ## formatEng(4100, siPrefix=true) == "4.1 k"
2737 ## formatEng(4.1, siPrefix=true, unit="") == "4.1 " # Space with unit=""
2738 ## formatEng(4100, siPrefix=true, unit="") == "4.1 k"
2739 ## formatEng(4100) == "4.1e3"
2740 ## formatEng(4100, unit="V") == "4.1e3 V"
2741 ## formatEng(4100, unit="", useUnitSpace=true) == "4.1e3 " # Space with useUnitSpace=true
2742 ## ```
2743 ##
2744 ## `decimalSep` is used as the decimal separator.
2745 ##
2746 ## See also:
2747 ## * `strformat module<strformat.html>`_ for string interpolation and formatting
2748 var
2749 absolute: BiggestFloat
2750 significand: BiggestFloat
2751 fexponent: BiggestFloat
2752 exponent: int
2753 splitResult: seq[string]
2754 suffix: string = ""
2755 func getPrefix(exp: int): char =
2756 ## Get the SI prefix for a given exponent
2757 ##
2758 ## Assumes exponent is a multiple of 3; returns ' ' if no prefix found
2759 const siPrefixes = ['a', 'f', 'p', 'n', 'u', 'm', ' ', 'k', 'M', 'G', 'T',
2760 'P', 'E']
2761 var index: int = (exp div 3) + 6
2762 result = ' '
2763 if index in low(siPrefixes)..high(siPrefixes):
2764 result = siPrefixes[index]
2765
2766 # Most of the work is done with the sign ignored, so get the absolute value
2767 absolute = abs(f)
2768 significand = f
2769
2770 if absolute == 0.0:
2771 # Simple case: just format it and force the exponent to 0
2772 exponent = 0
2773 result = significand.formatBiggestFloat(ffDecimal, precision,
2774 decimalSep = '.')
2775 else:
2776 # Find the best exponent that's a multiple of 3
2777 fexponent = floor(log10(absolute))
2778 fexponent = 3.0 * floor(fexponent / 3.0)
2779 # Adjust the significand for the new exponent
2780 significand /= pow(10.0, fexponent)
2781
2782 # Adjust the significand and check whether it has affected
2783 # the exponent
2784 absolute = abs(significand)
2785 if absolute >= 1000.0:
2786 significand *= 0.001
2787 fexponent += 3
2788 # Components of the result:
2789 result = significand.formatBiggestFloat(ffDecimal, precision,
2790 decimalSep = '.')
2791 exponent = fexponent.int()
2792
2793 splitResult = result.split('.')
2794 result = splitResult[0]
2795 # result should have at most one decimal character
2796 if splitResult.len() > 1:
2797 # If trim is set, we get rid of trailing zeros. Don't use trimZeros here as
2798 # we can be a bit more efficient through knowledge that there will never be
2799 # an exponent in this part.
2800 if trim:
2801 while splitResult[1].endsWith("0"):
2802 # Trim last character
2803 splitResult[1].setLen(splitResult[1].len-1)
2804 if splitResult[1].len() > 0:
2805 result &= decimalSep & splitResult[1]
2806 else:
2807 result &= decimalSep & splitResult[1]
2808
2809 # Combine the results accordingly
2810 if siPrefix and exponent != 0:
2811 var p = getPrefix(exponent)
2812 if p != ' ':
2813 suffix = " " & p
2814 exponent = 0 # Exponent replaced by SI prefix
2815 if suffix == "" and useUnitSpace:
2816 suffix = " "
2817 suffix &= unit
2818 if exponent != 0:
2819 result &= "e" & $exponent
2820 result &= suffix
2821
2822 ✗ func findNormalized(x: string, inArray: openArray[string]): int =
2823 ✗ var i = 0
2824 ✗ while i < high(inArray):
2825 ✗ if cmpIgnoreStyle(x, inArray[i]) == 0: return i
2826 ✗ inc(i, 2) # incrementing by 1 would probably lead to a
2827 # security hole...
2828 ✗ return -1
2829
2830 ✗ func invalidFormatString(formatstr: string) {.noinline.} =
2831 ✗ raise newException(ValueError, "invalid format string: " & formatstr)
2832 ✗
2833 ✗ func addf*(s: var string, formatstr: string, a: varargs[string, `$`]) {.rtl,
2834 extern: "nsuAddf".} =
2835 ## The same as `add(s, formatstr % a)`, but more efficient.
2836 const PatternChars = {'a'..'z', 'A'..'Z', '0'..'9', '\128'..'\255', '_'}
2837 var i = 0
2838 ✗ var num = 0
2839 ✗ while i < len(formatstr):
2840 ✗ if formatstr[i] == '$' and i+1 < len(formatstr):
2841 ✗ case formatstr[i+1]
2842 ✗ of '#':
2843 ✗ if num > a.high: invalidFormatString(formatstr)
2844 ✗ add s, a[num]
2845 ✗ inc i, 2
2846 ✗ inc num
2847 ✗ of '$':
2848 ✗ add s, '$'
2849 ✗ inc(i, 2)
2850 ✗ of '1'..'9', '-':
2851 var j = 0
2852 ✗ inc(i) # skip $
2853 var negative = formatstr[i] == '-'
2854 ✗ if negative: inc i
2855 ✗ while i < formatstr.len and formatstr[i] in Digits:
2856 ✗ j = j * 10 + ord(formatstr[i]) - ord('0')
2857 ✗ inc(i)
2858 ✗ let idx = if not negative: j-1 else: a.len-j
2859 ✗ if idx < 0 or idx > a.high: invalidFormatString(formatstr)
2860 ✗ add s, a[idx]
2861 ✗ of '{':
2862 ✗ var j = i+2
2863 ✗ var k = 0
2864 var negative = formatstr[j] == '-'
2865 ✗ if negative: inc j
2866 ✗ var isNumber = 0
2867 ✗ while j < formatstr.len and formatstr[j] notin {'\0', '}'}:
2868 ✗ if formatstr[j] in Digits:
2869 ✗ k = k * 10 + ord(formatstr[j]) - ord('0')
2870 ✗ if isNumber == 0: isNumber = 1
2871 ✗ else:
2872 ✗ isNumber = -1
2873 ✗ inc(j)
2874 ✗ if isNumber == 1:
2875 ✗ let idx = if not negative: k-1 else: a.len-k
2876 ✗ if idx < 0 or idx > a.high: invalidFormatString(formatstr)
2877 ✗ add s, a[idx]
2878 ✗ else:
2879 ✗ var x = findNormalized(substr(formatstr, i+2, j-1), a)
2880 ✗ if x >= 0 and x < high(a): add s, a[x+1]
2881 ✗ else: invalidFormatString(formatstr)
2882 ✗ i = j+1
2883 ✗ of 'a'..'z', 'A'..'Z', '\128'..'\255', '_':
2884 ✗ var j = i+1
2885 ✗ while j < formatstr.len and formatstr[j] in PatternChars: inc(j)
2886 ✗ var x = findNormalized(substr(formatstr, i+1, j-1), a)
2887 ✗ if x >= 0 and x < high(a): add s, a[x+1]
2888 ✗ else: invalidFormatString(formatstr)
2889 ✗ i = j
2890 ✗ else:
2891 ✗ invalidFormatString(formatstr)
2892 ✗ else:
2893 ✗ add s, formatstr[i]
2894 ✗ inc(i)
2895
2896 ✗ func `%`*(formatstr: string, a: openArray[string]): string {.rtl,
2897 extern: "nsuFormatOpenArray".} =
2898 ✗ ## Interpolates a format string with the values from `a`.
2899 ✗ ##
2900 ## The `substitution`:idx: operator performs string substitutions in
2901 ## `formatstr` and returns a modified `formatstr`. This is often called
2902 ## `string interpolation`:idx:.
2903 ##
2904 ## This is best explained by an example:
2905 ##
2906 ## ```nim
2907 ## "$1 eats $2." % ["The cat", "fish"]
2908 ## ```
2909 ##
2910 ## Results in:
2911 ##
2912 ## ```nim
2913 ## "The cat eats fish."
2914 ## ```
2915 ##
2916 ## The substitution variables (the thing after the `$`) are enumerated
2917 ## from 1 to `a.len`.
2918 ## To produce a verbatim `$`, use `$$`.
2919 ## The notation `$#` can be used to refer to the next substitution
2920 ## variable:
2921 ##
2922 ## ```nim
2923 ## "$# eats $#." % ["The cat", "fish"]
2924 ## ```
2925 ##
2926 ## Substitution variables can also be words (that is
2927 ## `[A-Za-z_]+[A-Za-z0-9_]*`) in which case the arguments in `a` with even
2928 ## indices are keys and with odd indices are the corresponding values.
2929 ## An example:
2930 ##
2931 ## ```nim
2932 ## "$animal eats $food." % ["animal", "The cat", "food", "fish"]
2933 ## ```
2934 ##
2935 ## Results in:
2936 ##
2937 ## ```nim
2938 ## "The cat eats fish."
2939 ## ```
2940 ##
2941 ## The variables are compared with `cmpIgnoreStyle`. `ValueError` is
2942 ## raised if an ill-formed format string has been passed to the `%` operator.
2943 ##
2944 ## See also:
2945 ## * `strformat module<strformat.html>`_ for string interpolation and formatting
2946 ✗ result = newStringOfCap(formatstr.len + a.len shl 4)
2947 ✗ addf(result, formatstr, a)
2948 ✗
2949 ✗ func `%`*(formatstr, a: string): string {.rtl,
2950 extern: "nsuFormatSingleElem".} =
2951 ## This is the same as `formatstr % [a]` (see
2952 ## `% func<#%25,string,openArray[string]>`_).
2953 result = newStringOfCap(formatstr.len + a.len)
2954 addf(result, formatstr, [a])
2955
2956 func format*(formatstr: string, a: varargs[string, `$`]): string {.rtl,
2957 extern: "nsuFormatVarargs".} =
2958 ## This is the same as `formatstr % a` (see
2959 ## `% func<#%25,string,openArray[string]>`_) except that it supports
2960 ## auto stringification.
2961 ##
2962 ## See also:
2963 ## * `strformat module<strformat.html>`_ for string interpolation and formatting
2964 result = newStringOfCap(formatstr.len + a.len)
2965 addf(result, formatstr, a)
2966
2967
2968 func strip*(s: string, leading = true, trailing = true,
2969 chars: set[char] = Whitespace): string {.rtl, extern: "nsuStrip".} =
2970 ## Strips leading or trailing `chars` (default: whitespace characters)
2971 ## from `s` and returns the resulting string.
2972 ##
2973 ## If `leading` is true (default), leading `chars` are stripped.
2974 ## If `trailing` is true (default), trailing `chars` are stripped.
2975 ## If both are false, the string is returned unchanged.
2976 ##
2977 ## See also:
2978 ## * `strip proc<strbasics.html#strip,string,set[char]>`_ Inplace version.
2979 ## * `stripLineEnd func<#stripLineEnd,string>`_
2980 runnableExamples:
2981 let a = " vhellov "
2982 let b = strip(a)
2983 doAssert b == "vhellov"
2984
2985 doAssert a.strip(leading = false) == " vhellov"
2986 doAssert a.strip(trailing = false) == "vhellov "
2987
2988 doAssert b.strip(chars = {'v'}) == "hello"
2989 doAssert b.strip(leading = false, chars = {'v'}) == "vhello"
2990
2991 let c = "blaXbla"
2992 doAssert c.strip(chars = {'b', 'a'}) == "laXbl"
2993 doAssert c.strip(chars = {'b', 'a', 'l'}) == "X"
2994
2995 var
2996 first = 0
2997 last = len(s)-1
2998 if leading:
2999 while first <= last and s[first] in chars: inc(first)
3000 if trailing:
3001 while last >= first and s[last] in chars: dec(last)
3002 result = substr(s, first, last)
3003
3004 func stripLineEnd*(s: var string) =
3005 ## Strips one of these suffixes from `s` in-place:
3006 ## `\r, \n, \r\n, \f, \v` (at most once instance).
3007 ## For example, can be useful in conjunction with `osproc.execCmdEx`.
3008 ## aka: `chomp`:idx:
3009 runnableExamples:
3010 var s = "foo\n\n"
3011 s.stripLineEnd
3012 doAssert s == "foo\n"
3013 s = "foo\r\n"
3014 s.stripLineEnd
3015 doAssert s == "foo"
3016
3017 if s.len > 0:
3018 case s[^1]
3019 of '\n':
3020 if s.len > 1 and s[^2] == '\r':
3021 s.setLen s.len-2
3022 else:
3023 s.setLen s.len-1
3024 of '\r', '\v', '\f':
3025 s.setLen s.len-1
3026 else:
3027 discard
3028
3029
3030 iterator tokenize*(s: string, seps: set[char] = Whitespace): tuple[
3031 token: string, isSep: bool] =
3032 ## Tokenizes the string `s` into substrings.
3033 ##
3034 ## Substrings are separated by a substring containing only `seps`.
3035 ## Example:
3036 ##
3037 ## ```nim
3038 ## for word in tokenize(" this is an example "):
3039 ## writeLine(stdout, word)
3040 ## ```
3041 ##
3042 ## Results in:
3043 ##
3044 ## ```nim
3045 ## (" ", true)
3046 ## ("this", false)
3047 ## (" ", true)
3048 ## ("is", false)
3049 ## (" ", true)
3050 ## ("an", false)
3051 ## (" ", true)
3052 ## ("example", false)
3053 ## (" ", true)
3054 ## ```
3055 var i = 0
3056 while true:
3057 var j = i
3058 var isSep = j < s.len and s[j] in seps
3059 while j < s.len and (s[j] in seps) == isSep: inc(j)
3060 if j > i:
3061 yield (substr(s, i, j-1), isSep)
3062 else:
3063 break
3064 i = j
3065
3066 func isEmptyOrWhitespace*(s: string): bool {.rtl,
3067 extern: "nsuIsEmptyOrWhitespace".} =
3068 ## Checks if `s` is empty or consists entirely of whitespace characters.
3069 result = s.allCharsInSet(Whitespace)
3070
Created using GCOVR (Version 8.5) at 2026-06-06 00:35:57
